title: gagea sylvatica
intro: Gagea sylvatica, also known as the woodlouse or forest gagea, is a perennial plant that belongs to the lily family (Liliaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve inflammation, and act as an expectorant. The health benefits of Gagea sylvatica include its potential to reduce fever, treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and alleviate digestive problems like indigestion and constipation. Gagea sylvatica is typically prepared as a tincture or decoction, and its herbal preparations are often used in combination with other plants to create a treatment for various health conditions.
